layoutRegions.name: masthead
layoutRegions.purpose: Summarize the conditional access simulation and expose the dry-run action.

layoutRegions.name: rule-panel
layoutRegions.purpose: Inspect conditional access rules with target apps, evaluated signals, outcomes, and confidence.

layoutRegions.name: access-matrix
layoutRegions.purpose: Compare actor, app, device, identity, and path signals before staging a policy change.

layoutRegions.name: simulation-panels
layoutRegions.purpose: Review break-glass exceptions, owner intent, rollback notes, and blocked paths.

components.name: RuleCard
components.purpose: Shows one conditional access rule with its target, evaluated signal, confidence, and allow, step-up, or block state.
interactions: state is visible through tokenized border color
card content stays stable across responsive breakpoints

components.name: ScenarioRow
components.purpose: Compares device posture, identity risk, and access path signals for a simulated actor.
interactions: grid collapses without hiding any signal

components.name: ExceptionCard
components.purpose: Captures break-glass window, owner intent, guardrails, and rollback-safe exception status.
interactions: expired or unowned exceptions are visually distinct

acceptanceChecks: Generated app imports src/omarchy-theme.css before app styles.
Every visible color comes from a --omarchy-* CSS variable or color-mix using one.
Every button, row, input, and command item has a visible :focus-visible state.
Layout remains stable at 320px, 768px, and 1280px viewport widths.
Primary content is usable on first load without a marketing interstitial.
A .desktop launcher can be generated with omarchy-native app desktop.
An opt-in theme sync hook can be generated with omarchy-native app hook.
